questions.question: Explain the concept of division of labour in multicellular organisms.
questions.answer: Division of labour means different groups of cells perform specific functions. This increases efficiency and helps organisms perform complex activities.

questions.question: Differentiate between unicellular and multicellular organisms.
questions.answer: Unicellular organisms have a single cell performing all functions, whereas multicellular organisms have many cells organized into tissues and organs.

questions.question: Write two characteristics of meristematic tissue.
questions.answer: Cells divide continuously and have dense cytoplasm with a prominent nucleus.

questions.question: Differentiate between apical and lateral meristem.
questions.answer: Apical meristem increases length of roots and stems, while lateral meristem increases thickness or girth.

questions.question: Explain the structure of parenchyma tissue.
questions.answer: Parenchyma cells are living, thin-walled, loosely packed and have large vacuoles.

questions.question: What are the functions of collenchyma?
questions.answer: Collenchyma provides support and flexibility to growing plant parts.

questions.question: Describe sclerenchyma tissue.
questions.answer: Sclerenchyma cells are dead with thick lignified walls that provide strength.

questions.question: What are complex permanent tissues?
questions.answer: They are tissues composed of different types of cells working together to perform a common function.

questions.question: Write the functions of xylem.
questions.answer: Xylem transports water and minerals and provides mechanical support.

questions.question: Write the functions of phloem.
questions.answer: Phloem transports prepared food from leaves to other plant parts.

questions.question: Explain the role of epidermis in plants.
questions.answer: Epidermis protects plant parts and reduces water loss with the help of cuticle.

questions.question: What is the function of stomata?
questions.answer: Stomata help in gaseous exchange and transpiration.

questions.question: Define epithelial tissue with its function.
questions.answer: Epithelial tissue forms protective coverings and lines organs and cavities.

questions.question: Give two functions of connective tissue.
questions.answer: Connective tissue supports organs and connects different body parts.

questions.question: Differentiate between tendon and ligament.
questions.answer: Tendon connects muscle to bone while ligament connects bone to bone.

questions.question: Explain adipose tissue.
questions.answer: Adipose tissue stores fat and acts as an insulator.

questions.question: Write the functions of bone tissue.
questions.answer: Bone tissue provides support and protects internal organs.

questions.question: Explain muscular tissue.
questions.answer: Muscular tissue contracts and relaxes to cause movement.

questions.question: Name the types of muscular tissue.
questions.answer: Striated, smooth and cardiac muscles.

questions.question: Describe cardiac muscle.
questions.answer: Cardiac muscles are branched involuntary muscles found in the heart.

questions.question: Define nervous tissue.
questions.answer: Nervous tissue receives stimuli and transmits impulses throughout the body.

questions.question: What is a neuron?
questions.answer: Neuron is the structural and functional unit of the nervous system.

questions.question: Write the parts of a neuron.
questions.answer: Cell body, dendrites and axon.

questions.question: What is the function of axon?
questions.answer: Axon carries nerve impulses away from the cell body.

questions.question: Explain the role of nervous tissue in coordination.
questions.answer: Nervous tissue transmits signals rapidly to coordinate body activities.
